gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Convert an integral-valued finite `f64` to `i64`, refusing values outside the"] # [doc = " exactly-representable `i64` range (C1 — never a silent saturation/truncation)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Rust's `f64 as i64` *saturates* (since 1.45): `1e20_f64 as i64 == i64::MAX`. A finite"] # [doc = " `f64` therefore passes `is_finite()` yet maps to a wrong, silent value. We reject it."] # [doc = " `i64::MIN` is exactly representable as `f64` (`-2^63`); `i64::MAX` is **not** (it rounds"] # [doc = " to `2^63`), so the honest upper bound is \"strictly below `2^63`\"."] fn checked_round_to_i64 (r : f64) -> Result < i64 , MathErr > { const I64_MIN_F64 : f64 = - 9_223_372_036_854_775_808.0 ; const TWO_POW_63 : f64 = 9_223_372_036_854_775_808.0 ; if ! (I64_MIN_F64 .. TWO_POW_63) . contains (& r) { return Err (MathErr :: Overflow) ; } Ok (r as i64) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " `floor(x)` — round toward negative infinity (exact under the `Floor` mode)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — the floor of an in-range finite `f64` is representable and exact."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`MathErr::OutOfDomain`] when `x` is NaN or infinite."] # [doc = " - [`MathErr::Overflow`] when `floor(x)` lies outside the `i64` range (never a silent clamp)."] pub fn floor (x : f64) -> Result < i64 , MathErr > { if ! x . is_finite () { return Err (MathErr :: OutOfDomain) ; } checked_round_to_i64 (x . floor ()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " `round(x, mode)` — round `x` to the nearest integer under the named, reified [`RoundMode`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`** — the rounded value is the exact image of `x` under `mode`. # [doc = " `gcd(a, b)` — greatest common divisor (always non-negative)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Guarantee: `Exact`; fallible on overflow.** Uses the binary GCD (Stein's algorithm) on the"] # [doc = " absolute values. `gcd(0, 0) = 0` (conventional)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Errors"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - [`MathErr::Overflow`] when the true gcd is `2^63` — i.e. when both inputs are drawn from"] # [doc = "   `{0, i64::MIN}` with at least one `i64::MIN` (e.g. `gcd(0, i64::MIN)`). `|i64::MIN| = 2^63`"] # [doc = "   is not representable in `i64`, so it is refused rather than wrapped to a negative value (C1)."] pub fn gcd (a : i64 , b : i64) -> Result < i64 , MathErr > { let mut a = a . unsigned_abs () ; let mut b = b . unsigned_abs () ; let result : u64 = if a == 0 { b } else if b == 0 { a } else { let shift = (a | b) . trailing_zeros () ; a >>= a . trailing_zeros () ; loop { b >>= b . trailing_zeros () ; if a > b { core :: mem :: swap (& mut a , & mut b) ; } b -= a ; if b == 0 { break ; } } a << shift } ; i64 :: try_from (result) . map_err (| _ | MathErr :: Overflow) }

(ratio (i64 :: MIN , - 1) , Err (MathErr :: Overflow)) ; } # [test] fn round_mode_is_echoed_back_as_explain_artifact () { for mode in [RoundMode :: Floor , RoundMode :: Ceil , RoundMode :: TruncTowardZero , RoundMode :: HalfAwayFromZero , RoundMode :: HalfToEven ,] { let (_ , returned_mode) = round (2.5 , mode) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (returned_mode , mode , "mode must be echoed back") ; } } # [test] fn round_half_even_rounds_to_even () { assert_eq ! (round (0.5 , RoundMode :: HalfToEven) . unwrap () . 0 , 0) ; assert_eq ! (round (1.5 , RoundMode :: HalfToEven) . unwrap () . 0 , 2) ; assert_eq ! (round (2.5 , RoundMode :: HalfToEven) . unwrap () . 0 , 2) ; assert_eq ! (round (- 0.5 , RoundMode :: HalfToEven) . unwrap () . 0 , 0) ; assert_eq ! (round (- 1.5 , RoundMode :: HalfToEven) . unwrap () . 0 , - 2) ; } # [test] fn round_half_away_from_zero () { assert_eq ! (round (0.5 , RoundMode :: HalfAwayFromZero) . unwrap () . 0 , 1) ; assert_eq ! (round (1.5 , RoundMode :: HalfAwayFromZero) . unwrap () . 0 , 2) ; assert_eq ! (round (- 0.5 , RoundMode :: HalfAwayFromZero) . unwrap () . 0 , - 1) ; } # [test] fn round_nan_is_explicit_error () { assert_eq ! (round (f64 :: NAN , RoundMode :: Floor) , Err (MathErr :: OutOfDomain)) ; } }
